description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring several large, rectangular photovoltaic panels mounted on a broad, flat base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, slightly tilted configuration, likely to optimize sunlight capture. The base appears to be metallic or concrete, with a light blue-gray surface and black structural supports at intervals. There are additional box-like components, possibly housing electrical equipment or batteries, distributed along the base. The overall structure is elongated, with the solar panels occupying the central and rear sections, while the front section includes a sloped, metallic element that may serve as a protective or aerodynamic feature. The installation is designed for stationary placement, suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or dedicated solar farms.
description: A large, elongated solar energy installation with multiple tilted photovoltaic panels on a flat, blue-gray base and several black support structures.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the installation, with the tilted solar panels and black support structures clearly visible along the base.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the staggered arrangement of the solar panels and the depth of the base, with the sloped metallic feature at the far end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the linear arrangement of the panels and the distribution of black box-like components along the base.
description_view_3: Reverse end view focusing on the alignment and tilt of the solar panels, as well as the spacing between the support structures.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with visible aluminum or steel frame supports
attribute_array_configuration: multiple rows of panels in a staggered linear arrangement, approximately 4-6 panels total
